[2018-02-15 05:05] redrame: "don't buy it" is far from the worst heuristic for it
[2018-02-15 05:05] redrame: blood pact is a last resort or it's tempo defense against burst
[2018-02-15 05:05] redrame: or it's a way to punish breachproof
[2018-02-15 05:06] redrame: because against breachproof you can just kill the grimbotch

[2018-02-15 05:06] mrguy888: It also punishes synced scorches or isos.
[2018-02-15 05:07] redrame: you wouldn't never breachproof but you'd be highly inclined not to
[2018-02-15 05:07] redrame: breachproofing is already something you're inclined not to do by the mechanics of absorb
[2018-02-15 05:08] redrame: blood pact is kind of like a red aegis against breachproof, if you think that a set is so obviously breachproof that you can deal with that then go for it

[2018-02-15 05:11] redrame: they mostly look ok on a quick run through
[2018-02-15 05:11] redrame: generally you buy blood pact when it feels like you dont have a choice outside holding drones

[2018-02-15 05:12] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): ah ok, that's a good metric
[2018-02-15 05:12] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): if the alternative is holding drones, blood pact is more efficient. that was mainly my thought that game as well
[2018-02-15 05:13] redrame: blood pact is actually better than holding 3 drones by a pretty wide margin too
[2018-02-15 05:13] redrame: that's not exactly a praise for the unit
[2018-02-15 05:14] redrame: but if one side buys blood pact and the other holds drones the side that holds drones will have a bad time
[2018-02-15 05:14] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): so you're giving up 4 damage over 4 turns or 3 damage over 3 turns and 2 soak for 4 soak basically right?
[2018-02-15 05:15] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): so if you're really closing out a game it's an ok trade-off, too?
[2018-02-15 05:15] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): or if it forces your opponent to make the same play
[2018-02-15 05:16] redrame: blood pact is also granular, occasionally that makes the difference in buying it earlier than you normally would
[2018-02-15 05:16] redrame: if blood pact stops you from getting exploited by your opponent's attack count then you're really paying for 5+ defense instead of 4
[2018-02-15 05:17] redrame: you would rather buy husk if you just need granular defense though
[2018-02-15 05:18] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Cool. That helps me understand a bit more
[2018-02-15 05:18] apooche: blood pact is also a little better when you can chill because chill generates value vs grimbotch
[2018-02-15 05:18] redrame: ok I see some pacts where you should be doing other things now
[2018-02-15 05:18] redrame: for example, you seem to be clicking your doomed mechs every single turn
[2018-02-15 05:19] redrame: doomed mech can either be 2 attack or 2 absorb greater than wall each turn, but there's a difference there since holding the doomed mech means you can spend less gold on defense and more gold on attack
[2018-02-15 05:19] redrame: so in general you should slightly favor defending on doomed mech if it will absorb for 4
[2018-02-15 05:20] redrame: that is extra true when you are otherwise buying blood pact
[2018-02-15 05:20] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): yeah that makes sense now
[2018-02-15 05:20] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): my thought was i want to put as much attack pressure every turn since i'm forcing my opponent to buy blood pacts
[2018-02-15 05:21] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): but that's definitely wrong if im missing out on absorb and need to spend blood pacts myself
[2018-02-15 05:21] redrame: yeah, that would be good logic if you could defend without blood pact
[2018-02-15 05:22] redrame: but if you're just causing a wash then you might as well hold dmech since you have to buy the pact sooner than your opponent
[2018-02-15 05:25] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): cool, thanks! that helps me understand more. now to try to get out of tier 1 hell on Ranked :p
[2018-02-15 05:25] redrame: you'll get there, the guy that you beat was a master
[2018-02-15 05:25] apooche: the crazier it gets, the better holding back mech becomes
[2018-02-15 05:25] apooche: unless it is breaching

[2018-02-15 08:57] redrame: I think a unit hierarchy is probably a bad idea in every single way
[2018-02-15 08:58] redrame: attacker/absorber hierarchy at least felt like they had some potential in not being useless
[2018-02-15 08:58] redrame: but like compare tatsu+ematrix to tatsu in a thorium asteri set
[2018-02-15 08:58] redrame: you just get too many goofy situations to rank units
[2018-02-15 08:59] redrame: also you for sure shouldnt read in to what is the 44th vs 45th best unit whereas 1st vs 2nd best absorber at least has a little meaning
[2018-02-15 09:01] redrame: I lost a game where I went feral vs colossus today bc I wanted to test out 307ths tier list to see how well/poorly it holds up

[2018-02-15 14:09] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): My friend had a really weird reaction that I hadn't considered: "I probably won't play it because it looks like it would take a really long time to get good at it"  I tried to explain that learning was more fun than just being good, but he wasn't having it and went back to Hearthstone.
[2018-02-15 14:09] 307th: that's pretty interesting
[2018-02-15 14:09] 307th: i think that's about accessibility
[2018-02-15 14:10] wilson0862 (Wilson): Also if they went back to HS...
[2018-02-15 14:10] 307th: like, some games aren't really fun until you've learned a lot, whereas some games are fun to play after a pretty short time
[2018-02-15 14:10] 307th: so, like, i haven't gotten into dota2 or league of legends because it feels like it'd take a long time before i'd learned all the heroes
[2018-02-15 14:10] 307th: and i think the game wouldn't be fun until i knew that and other strategy stuff
[2018-02-15 14:11] 307th: imo prismata's pretty accessible, not sure if there's a good way to communicate that though
[2018-02-15 14:13] 307th: i guess the "easy to learn, impossible to master" saying about chess is getting at that
[2018-02-15 14:13] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Also for advertising, something about it being similar to chess but more about reacting than memorizing because of the advanced set changing. [2018-02-19 00:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Lol found another insignificant bug with regards to (I assume) the feathers package (Elyot said that's what's used for in-game chat and stuff). In chat when there is a scrollbar (because there is more text than can fit in the chatbox), if you scroll, and stop to wait until the scrollbar starts fading out, then very quickly just as it is fading out mouse over and click and hold, then the scrollbar won't be visible but will consider you to be holding it with the cursor, allowing you to drag to move it. Managed to fairly consistently reproduce it, though it requires a fraction of a second's timing

I guess I'll report it but it's just another feathers issue that they probably can't do anything about and also I doubt anyone would ever care about this particular one ;P
